ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee (e.g., Pria, Wanita) */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the employee */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the employee (Y/N) */,
  "number_of_dependents" INTEGER /* Number of dependents the employee has */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest education level of the employee */,
  "gpa" REAL /* GPA of the employee */,
  "year_graduated" INTEGER /* Year the employee graduated */,
  "employee_type" TEXT /* Type of employee (e.g., Mantri Kupedes, Mantri Briguna) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "hiring_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was terminated (if applicable) */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the current department of the employee */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES positions ("department"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_levels" (
  "job_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job level */,
  "job_level_code" TEXT /* Code of the job level (e.g., JG04, JG05) */,
  "job_level_description" TEXT /* Description of the job level */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the job level */,
  "responsibilities" TEXT /* Responsibilities associated with the job level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "personal_levels" (
  "personal_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each personal level */,
  "personal_level_code" TEXT /* Code of the personal level (e.g., PG03, PG06) */,
  "personal_level_description" TEXT /* Description of the personal level */,
  "skills_required" TEXT /* Skills required for the personal level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("personal_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_assignments" (
  "job_assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job assignment */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "job_level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job level */,
  "personal_level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the personal level */,
  "branch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the branch */,
  "job_duration_in_current_job_level" REAL /* Duration in the current job level (years) */,
  "job_duration_in_current_person_level" REAL /* Duration in the current personal level (years) */,
  "job_duration_in_current_branch" REAL /* Duration in the current branch (years) */,
  "job_duration_from_training" INTEGER /* Duration since the last training (years) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the assignment */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the assignment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the assignment (e.g., active, completed) */,
  "performance_rating" REAL /* Performance rating of the employee during the assignment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_assignments_branch_id FOREIGN KEY ("branch_id") REFERENCES branches ("branch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_assignments_job_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_level_id") REFERENCES job_levels ("job_level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_assignments_personal_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("personal_level_id") REFERENCES personal_levels ("personal_level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_assignments_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "branches" (
  "branch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each branch */,
  "branch_name" TEXT /* Name of the branch */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the branch */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the branch */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the branch manager */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of employees the branch can handle */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("branch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_branches_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_records" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "annual_leave" INTEGER /* Number of annual leave days taken */,
  "sick_leaves" INTEGER /* Number of sick leave days taken */,
  "last_achievement_percentage" REAL /* Last achievement percentage */,
  "achievements_above_100_percent" INTEGER /* Number of achievements above 100% in the last three quarters */,
  "best_performance" INTEGER /* Best performance indicator (1 for best, 0 for not) */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance review */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the person who conducted the review */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or feedback from the performance review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_records_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_records_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_rotations" (
  "rotation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job rotation */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "from_branch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the branch the employee is rotating from */,
  "to_branch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the branch the employee is rotating to */,
  "from_job_level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job level the employee is rotating from */,
  "to_job_level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job level the employee is rotating to */,
  "rotation_date" TEXT /* Date of the rotation */,
  "rotation_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the rotation */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date the rotation was approved */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the person who approved the rotation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rotation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_rotations_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_rotations_to_job_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("to_job_level_id") REFERENCES job_levels ("job_level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_rotations_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_rotations_from_branch_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_branch_id") REFERENCES branches ("branch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_rotations_to_branch_id FOREIGN KEY ("to_branch_id") REFERENCES branches ("branch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_rotations_from_job_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_job_level_id") REFERENCES job_levels ("job_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training_records" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "training_name" TEXT /* Name of the training program */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date the training was completed */,
  "training_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the training (in hours) */,
  "training_provider" TEXT /* Provider of the training */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the training */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certification earned from the training */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_records_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignment */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "position_id" INTEGER /* ID of the assigned position */,
  "assignment_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the assignment */,
  "assignment_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the assignment */,
  "assignment_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the assignment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the assignment (e.g., active, completed) */,
  "performance_rating" REAL /* Performance rating of the employee during the assignment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assignments_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assignments_position_id FOREIGN KEY ("position_id") REFERENCES positions ("position_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "positions" (
  "position_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each position */,
  "position_name" TEXT /* Name of the position */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the position belongs to */,
  "responsibilities" TEXT /* Responsibilities of the position */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the position */,
  "required_certifications" TEXT /* Certifications required for the position */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("position_id")
)
